name: Brad Garrett
photo_credit: Marc Levin via Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 2.0)
blurb: The voice — six-foot-eight, baritone, a working impressionist who won Star Search at 23 and never stopped being a road-headlining stand-up under the Robert Barone fame.
bio: Brad Garrett dropped out of UCLA in 1980 to start stand-up at LA's Improv and Pasadena Ice House. In 1984, at 23, he became the first $100,000 grand-champion winner of Star Search's comedy bracket — a televised win that earned him an early Johnny Carson spot and opening slots in Las Vegas for Frank Sinatra, Sammy Davis Jr., the Beach Boys, Diana Ross, and Liza Minnelli. He kept headlining Vegas through the impressionist-and-baritone years even as TV roles started piling up (the voice of Hulk Hogan in Hulk Hogan's Rock 'n' Wrestling, a Seinfeld 'Bottle Deposit' mechanic, eventually Robert Barone on Everybody Loves Raymond from 1996–2005). He still works rooms — in June 2010 he opened Brad Garrett's Comedy Club at the Tropicana, moved it across the street to the MGM Grand in 2011, and headlines there regularly. His stand-up is the older, Catskills-into-Carson lineage of impressions and crowd-work; the Emmy-winning sitcom run came after.

timeline.ev: Born April 14 (as Brad H. Gerstenfeld) in Oxnard, California
Drops out of UCLA to pursue stand-up at LA's Improv and the Ice House (Pasadena)
First $100,000 grand-champion winner in the comedy category on Star Search; debut on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson
Voice of Hulk Hogan in Hulk Hogan's Rock 'n' Wrestling (1985–86)
Cast as Robert Barone on Everybody Loves Raymond — runs nine seasons through 2005
Wins first Primetime Emmy for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series (Raymond); wins again in 2003 and 2005
Emmy-nominated lead performance as Jackie Gleason in the TV film Gleason
Opens Brad Garrett's Comedy Club at the Tropicana Las Vegas
Closes the Tropicana club; re-opens Brad Garrett's Comedy Club at the MGM Grand
Co-creates Disney+'s Big Shot with David E. Kelley and Dean Lorey

accolades: Star Search Comedy Grand Champion — first $100,000 winner in the category (1984)
Three Primetime Emmy Awards for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series (2002, 2003, 2005)
Emmy nomination for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Miniseries or Movie (Gleason, 2003)
2003 Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by an Ensemble in a Comedy Series (Everybody Loves Raymond)
Headliner residency at Brad Garrett's Comedy Club, MGM Grand Las Vegas (2011–present)
